title: catha emarginata
intro: Catha emarginata, also known as Khat, is a flowering plant native to the Horn of Africa and the Arabian Peninsula, belonging to the Celastraceae family. The leaves of the Catha emarginata plant contain cathinone, a stimulant alkaloid with medicinal properties, which has been used to treat various health issues such as fatigue, lethargy, and respiratory problems. The plant's leaves have been traditionally used for their stimulating and energizing effects, providing benefits such as increased alertness, improved mental clarity, and enhanced physical performance. Catha emarginata leaves can be prepared as a tea, smoked, or chewed, with the most common herbal preparation being a traditional tea made by infusing the leaves in hot water.
